articles.title: Hong Kong to seek administrative measures to protect same-sex couple rights
articles.content:
Hong Kong authorities are considering administrative measures to protect the rights of same-sex couples after a bill to recognise such partnerships was rejected by the legislature, the city leader has said.
Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu also said on Tuesday the ill-fated Registration of Same-sex Partnerships Bill had actually shown that the Legislative Council was not a “rubber stamp”, as some “unfairly” suggested.
Last week, Legco vetoed the government’s bill, which would have recognised...

articles.title: Students’ rights come first: Hong Kong’s John Lee on HKIS-LCMS legal dispute
articles.content:
Hong Kong’s leader has called on a top international school and its US-based founding organisation, currently embroiled in a legal dispute, to prioritise quality education and protect the interests of students.
Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu on Tuesday also urged the operator of the Hong Kong International School (HKIS) to update parents and students of the latest developments regularly.
Last week, the Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod (LCMS), which helped found HKIS, dropped a bombshell by suing

articles.title: Mother of wanted Hong Kong activist Hau Chung-yu summoned for police questioning
articles.content:
The fourth activist wanted for taking part in an unofficial election organised by the “subversive” Hong Kong Parliament has had an immediate family member summoned for police questioning, the Post has learned.
A source familiar with the matter told the Post that Hau Chung-yu’s mother, 56, had been summoned to Tsuen Wan police station to assist with investigations.
“She is expected to leave the police station at noon,” the source said.
Tuesday’s police probe marked the fourth occasion on which...

articles.title: Hong Kong policy address to focus on reforms, economy and livelihoods: John Lee
articles.content:
Hong Kong’s leader has announced that his policy address, to be delivered on Wednesday, will focus on deepening reforms, boosting the economy and enhancing livelihoods while leveraging the city’s strengths.
Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu also said on Tuesday that he chose to keep green as the theme colour, adding that it symbolised vitality and sustainability, and reflected the city’s continuing vibrancy for a “brighter future”.
“The theme of the policy address is deepening reforms for our...

articles.title: Hong Kong must update its sexual offence laws to empower survivors
articles.content:
As Hong Kong prepares for a likely public consultation on reforming the Crimes Ordinance, the urgent need to update our outdated sexual offence laws has never been clearer. Just months ago, in July, a scandal at the University of Hong Kong erupted when a law student allegedly used artificial intelligence (AI) to create deepfake pornography targeting over 20 students and teachers, exposing the city’s legal vulnerabilities.
This is not an isolated issue. Hong Kong’s sexual offence laws date back..

articles.title: 2022 Beijing Games launching worldwide search for volunteers
articles.content: Beijing is launching an 18-month global recruitment drive as it looks to hire around 27,000 volunteers to work at the 2022 Winter Olympics.

articles.title: Li raring to make most of Melbourne matchup
articles.content: Li Haotong says he will be bursting with pride when he becomes the first Chinese mainland golfer to play in the Presidents Cup next week.

articles.title: On chinadaily.com.cn
articles.content: Tech: Cameras to spot drivers using phones

articles.title: Good stories give us a welcome break from world troubles
articles.content: Sometimes we all get tired of hearing the news from the real world - trade wars, political machinations and slowing economies. So, it is great to get immersed in a good long story.

articles.title: This Day, That Year
articles.content: Editor's note: This year marks the 70th anniversary of the founding of New China.

articles.title: China-US trade & tariff developments
articles.content: Chinese and US delegations convened on Sunday for talks on economic and trade issues. Over the past few months, the two teams met in Geneva, London, and Stockholm, achieving some positive results.

articles.title: Iranian ambassador: SCO cooperation is fruitful
articles.content: Since Iran officially became a member state of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) in 2023, it has been actively cooperating with relevant countries in various fields within the SCO framework and has achieved fruitful results, said Abdolreza Rahmani Fazli, Iranian ambassador to China, in an interview with China Media Group.

articles.title: China's summer box office earnings surpass same period in 2024
articles.content: China's summer box office earnings surpass same period in 2024

articles.title: Foreign capital flocks back to China's capital markets
articles.content: A notable shift in global capital flows is underway, with international investors increasingly turning their attention back to China's capital markets. After a prolonged period of outflows, these markets have experienced sustained foreign inflows since early summer, signaling renewed confidence in Chinese assets.

articles.title: China, Pakistan to welcome 75th anniversary of diplomatic ties in 2026
articles.content: China, Pakistan to welcome 75th anniversary of diplomatic ties in 2026

articles.title: S.Korean court extends detention of ex-president Park over corruption charges
articles.content: A South Korean court on Friday decided to extend the detention of impeached President Park Geun-hye who has been in custody and stood trial over corruption charges including bribery.

articles.title: Egypt-U.S. joint drill Bright Star simulates real battles
articles.content: Conditions similar to real battles were created in the Egyptian-U.S. joint drill "Bright Star 2017," which are being conducted at Egypt's Mohamed Naguib Military Base and will last until Sept. 20, local media reported on Monday.

articles.title: MPs say Brexit referendum led to public mistrust in government
articles.content: Actions by the British government during the European Union referendum led to public mistrust, a committee of MPs said Wednesday in a parliamentary report.

articles.title: Mixed reaction on London streets to British triggering Article 50
articles.content: The formal triggering of Article 50 by Britain to quit the European Union (EU) on Wednesday drew mixed reactions on London streets.

articles.title: China accuses Nvidia of violating anti-monopoly laws
articles.content: The accusation comes as the US and China hold talks to de-escalate the trade war between the two.

articles.title: Nasa bans Chinese nationals from working on its space programmes
articles.content: Earlier this month, Chinese nationals working with Nasa suddenly found themselves denied access.

articles.title: Hong Kong lawmakers say no to more rights for same-sex couples
articles.content: The law would have recognised some rights for couples who were married overseas.

articles.title: Trump's tariffs are pushing food and drink exporters closer to China
articles.content: Agricultural brokers tell the BBC there has been a surge of interest from exporters in trade with China.

articles.title: China's Xi steals the limelight in a defiant push against US-led world order
articles.content: President Xi shows off his power and influence in an open challenge to US supremacy and the world order.

articles.title: Fosun’s Henlius Said in Talks With J&J, Roche on Cancer Drug
articles.content: Shanghai Henlius Biotech Inc. is in talks with multinational pharmaceutical firms including Johnson & Johnson and Roche Holding AG about selling them the rights to an experimental cancer drug, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: Chinese Biotech Stock’s 4,500% Surge Sparks Fear of Market Froth
articles.content: A Chinese biotech firm has skyrocketed more than 4,500% in the three months since its Hong Kong debut, stoking worries of speculative buying.

articles.title: JPMorgan to Cut China, India Share in Flagship EM Bond Index
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase & Co. will cut the weight of the largest bond issuers in its flagship emerging-market index, diverting investor flows from the likes of China and India toward smaller nations.

articles.title: Senate Confirms Miran, Court Blocks Trump’s Bid To Fire Cook | The Asia Trade, 9/16/25
articles.content:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Hong Kong with Shery Ahn and Annabelle Droulers, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Chinese Lidar Maker Hesai Jumps in Hong Kong Debut
articles.content: Hesai Group, the world's largest maker of lidar sensors used in cars, soared in its trading debut in Hong Kong. The company's co-founder and CEO, David Li, says the company is also eyeing leadership in the robotics segment. He speaks with Stephen Engle on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
articles.content: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

articles.title: Kim Jong Un Is Doing Everything He Can to Keep North Korea's Youth in Line
articles.content: The dictator, eager to shut out the influence of Hollywood and K-pop, has been exalting a “shock brigade” of 300,000 teens and 20-somethings drafted into flood reconstruction.

articles.title: Germany's Economic Model Is Broken, and No One Has a Plan B
articles.content: The country is focused on exports, but China is slowing imports and U.S. tariff threats are growing. Politicians are offering few alternatives.

articles.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
articles.content: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

articles.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
articles.content: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

articles.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
articles.content:
In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction.

The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

articles.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
articles.content: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

articles.title: ‘I have to do it’: Why one of the world’s most brilliant AI scientists left the US for China
articles.content:
In 2020, after spending half his life in the US, Song-Chun Zhu took a one-way ticket to China. Now he might hold the key to who wins the global AI race

By the time Song-Chun Zhu was six years old, he had encountered death more times than he could count. Or so it felt. This was the early 1970s, the waning years of the Cultural Revolution, and his father ran a village supply store in rural China. There was little to do beyond till the fields and study Mao Zedong at home, and so the shop b

articles.title: The Guardian view on Merck’s exit: Britain’s biopharma strategy stalls in the face of China’s rise | Editorial
articles.content:
The industry’s retreat from the UK reflects a deeper shift about how Beijing is rewriting the rules of innovation

When Merck abruptly scrapped its billion-pound London research hub last week, critics blamed Britain’s lacklustre support for life sciences and a Scrooge-like grip on NHS drug prices. But one important factor may have been missed. That

articles.title: UK government ‘disappointed’ charges dropped against men accused of spying for China
articles.content:
Christopher Cash and Christopher Berry were due to go on trial next month charged with breaching Official Secrets Act

The UK government says it is “extremely disappointed” by a decision to drop charges of spying for China against two men, one of whom was a parliamentary aide.

The men, Christopher Cash, 30, and Christopher Berry, 33, had denied accusations of providing information prejudicial to the interests of the state in breach of the Official Secrets Act between December 2021

articles.title: US and China reach deal to transfer TikTok ownership, trade officials say
articles.content:
Framework deal is breakthrough in long-running dispute over app’s ownership amid security concerns in Washington

Jamieson Greer, a US trade representative, said on Monday that Washington and Beijing have struck a framework agreement on transferring TikTok to US-controlled ownership.

Speaking after emerging from negotiations with Chinese officials, Scott Bessent said th

articles.title: Britain to roll out red carpet, royal fanfare for Trump visit
articles.content: US President Donald Trump will be hosted to a state banquet by King Charles at Windsor Castle, and meet with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer at his country estate.

articles.title: What hurdles lie ahead for any US-China TikTok deal?
articles.content: Any agreement could require approval by the Republican-controlled Congress, which passed a law in 2024 requiring ByteDance to divest TikTok or face a ban in the US.

articles.title: China accuses Nvidia of violating anti-monopoly laws
articles.content: The claim came as the US and China held trade talks in Madrid that concluded on Monday.

articles.title: TikTok ban in flux as White House announces China-US framework deal
articles.content: The app would have gone dark if China’s ByteDance had not divested by Wednesday.

articles.title: Afghanistan's Taliban regime is waging 'war on all forms of secular education'
articles.content: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, September 15: While the US and the Taliban are developping "bilateral relations", papers report on the regime's "war" on secular education. Also: after pro-Palestinian protests cut the Vuelta cycling race short, an opinion piece says it's a "bad idea" to separate sports and politics. Finally, we look at some politically charged statements at the Emmys and breakthrough actor Owen Cooper's record win.

articles.title: Australia to invest $8 billion in nuclear sub shipyard
articles.content: Australia is investing Aus$12 billion (US$8 billion) over the next 10 years to transform a shipyard in Western Australia into a nuclear submarine service facility. The project is part of a major military overhaul aimed at strengthening Australia’s defences amid a growing threat from China.

articles.title: 'Years of frustration' caused Nepal unrest: Papers react to Gen Z protests
articles.content: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, September 10: Nepal's Gen Z protests cause several Nepali news sites to be inaccessible. But first: the French papers react to their new prime minister. Also, the inside pages in France attempt to understand this Wednesday's "Bloquons tout" movement and strikes. Finally, the Guardian brings us a bit of joy as conservationists build boats for endangered saltmarsh sparrows.

articles.title: EVs Cheaper To Operate Than Gas Cars, But Ownership Costs Remain High
articles.content: It's still cheaper to charge an EV or plug-in hybrid than it is to fill up at a gas station, but AAA found annual ownership cost remains higher for EV owners.

articles.title: Australia-PNG defence treaty: countries to agree to defend each other from military attack as China’s Pacific influence grows
articles.content:
Anthony Albanese and Papua New Guinea’s defence minister downplayed the delay to the Pukpuk defence treaty being formalised
